(L1) Ensure Administrative accounts are cloud-only
(L1) Ensure that between two and four global admins are designated
(L2) Ensure that only organizationally managed/approved public groups exist
(L1) Ensure sign-in to shared mailboxes is blocked
(L1) Ensure the 'Password expiration policy' is set to 'Set passwords to never expire (recommended)'
(L2) Ensure 'External sharing' of calendars is not available
(L2) Ensure the customer lockbox feature is enabled
(L2) Ensure Safe Links for Office Applications is Enabled (Only Checks Default Policy)
(L2) Ensure comprehensive attachment filtering is applied
(L1) Ensure the connection filter IP allow list is not used (Only Checks Default Policy)
(L1) Ensure the connection filter safe list is off (Only Checks Default Policy)
(L1) Ensure the Common Attachment Types Filter is enabled (Only Checks Default Policy)
(L1) Ensure notifications for internal users sending malware is Enabled (Only Checks Default Policy)
(L2) Ensure Safe Attachments policy is enabled (Only Checks Default Policy)
(L2) Ensure Safe Attachments for SharePoint, OneDrive, and Microsoft Teams is Enabled
(L1) Ensure Exchange Online Spam Policies are set to notify administrators (Only Checks Default Policy)
(L1) Ensure that an anti-phishing policy has been created (Only Checks Default Policy)
(L1) Ensure that DKIM is enabled for all Exchange Online Domains
(L1) Ensure Zero-hour auto purge for Microsoft Teams is on (Only Checks ZAP is enabled)
(L1) Ensure Microsoft 365 audit log search is Enabled
(L2) Ensure external file sharing in Teams is enabled for only approved cloud storage services
(L1) Ensure communication with unmanaged Teams users is disabled
(L1) Ensure communication with Skype users is disabled
(L1) Ensure all or a majority of third-party and custom apps are blocked
(L1) Ensure only people in my org can bypass the lobby
(L1) Ensure users can report security concerns in Teams to internal destination
Legacy authentication SHALL be blocked.
Users detected as high risk SHALL be blocked.
A notification SHOULD be sent to the administrator when high-risk users are detected.
Sign-ins detected as high risk SHALL be blocked.
Phishing-resistant MFA SHALL be enforced for all users.
If phishing-resistant MFA has not been enforced, an alternative MFA method SHALL be enforced for all users.
If Microsoft Authenticator is enabled, it SHALL be configured to show login context information.
The Authentication Methods Manage Migration feature SHALL be set to Migration Complete.
The authentication methods SMS, Voice Call, and Email One-Time Passcode (OTP) SHALL be disabled.
Phishing-resistant MFA SHALL be required for highly privileged roles.
Managed devices SHOULD be required for authentication.
Managed Devices SHOULD be required to register MFA.
Security logs SHALL be sent to the agency's security operations center for monitoring.
Only administrators SHALL be allowed to register applications.
Only administrators SHALL be allowed to consent to applications.
An admin consent workflow SHALL be configured for applications.
Group owners SHALL NOT be allowed to consent to applications.
User passwords SHALL NOT expire.
A minimum of two users and a maximum of eight users SHALL be provisioned with the Global Administrator role.
Privileged users SHALL be provisioned with finer-grained roles instead of Global Administrator.
Privileged users SHALL be provisioned cloud-only accounts separate from an on-premises directory or other federated identity providers.
Permanent active role assignments SHALL NOT be allowed for highly privileged roles.
Provisioning users to highly privileged roles SHALL NOT occur outside of a PAM system.
Activation of the Global Administrator role SHALL require approval.
Eligible and Active highly privileged role assignments SHALL trigger an alert.
User activation of the Global Administrator role SHALL trigger an alert.
User activation of other highly privileged roles SHOULD trigger an alert.
Guest users SHOULD have limited or restricted access to Azure AD directory objects.
Only users with the Guest Inviter role SHOULD be able to invite guest users.
Guest invites SHOULD only be allowed to specific external domains that have been authorized by the agency for legitimate business purposes.
DKIM SHOULD be enabled for all domains.
Contact folders SHALL NOT be shared with all domains.
Calendar details SHALL NOT be shared with all domains.
External sender warnings SHALL be implemented.
A DLP solution SHALL be used.
Disallowed file types SHALL be determined and enforced.
At a minimum, click-to-run files SHOULD be blocked (e.g., .exe, .cmd, and .vbe).
Automatic forwarding to external domains SHALL be disabled.
Emails SHALL be scanned for malware.
Emails identified as containing malware SHALL be quarantined or dropped.
Email scanning SHALL be capable of reviewing emails after delivery.
Impersonation protection checks SHOULD be used.
User warnings, comparable to the user safety tips included with EOP, SHOULD be displayed.
The phishing protection solution SHOULD include an AI-based phishing detection tool comparable to EOP Mailbox Intelligence.
IP allow lists SHOULD NOT be created.
Safe lists SHOULD NOT be enabled.
Mailbox auditing SHALL be enabled.
A spam filter SHALL be enabled.
Spam and high confidence spam SHALL be moved to either the junk email folder or the quarantine folder.
Allowed domains SHALL NOT be added to inbound anti-spam protection policies.
If a third-party party filtering solution is used, the solution SHOULD offer services comparable to the native spam filtering offered by Microsoft.
URL comparison with a block-list SHOULD be enabled.
Direct download links SHOULD be scanned for malware.
User click tracking SHOULD be enabled.
Alerts SHALL be enabled.
Alerts SHOULD be sent to a monitored address or incorporated into a security information and event management (SIEM) system.
Microsoft Purview Audit (Standard) logging SHALL be enabled.
Microsoft Purview Audit (Premium) logging SHALL be enabled.
Audit logs SHALL be maintained for at least the minimum duration dictated by OMB M-21-31 (Appendix C).
A list of approved IP addresses for sending mail SHALL be maintained.
An SPF policy SHALL be published for each domain, designating only these addresses as approved senders.
DKIM SHOULD be enabled for all domains.
A DMARC policy SHALL be published for every second-level domain.
The DMARC message rejection option SHALL be p=reject.
The DMARC point of contact for aggregate reports SHALL include reports@dmarc.cyber.dhs.gov.
SMTP AUTH SHALL be disabled.
Contact folders SHALL NOT be shared with all domains.
Calendar details SHALL NOT be shared with all domains.
External sender warnings SHALL be implemented.
A DLP solution SHALL be used.
The DLP solution SHALL protect personally identifiable information (PII) and sensitive information, as defined by the agency.
The selected DLP solution SHOULD offer services comparable to the native DLP solution offered by Microsoft.
At a minimum, the DLP solution SHALL restrict sharing credit card numbers, U.S. Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers (ITIN), and U.S. Social Security numbers (SSN) via email.
Emails SHALL be filtered by attachment file types.
The attachment filter SHOULD attempt to determine the true file type and assess the file extension.
Disallowed file types SHALL be determined and enforced.
Alternatively chosen filtering solutions SHOULD offer services comparable to Microsoft Defender's Common Attachment Filter.
At a minimum, click-to-run files SHOULD be blocked (e.g., .exe, .cmd, and .vbe).
External sharing for SharePoint SHALL be limited to Existing guests or Only People in your organization.
External sharing SHALL be restricted to approved external domains and/or users in approved security groups per interagency collaboration needs.
Ensure no user has permanent access to all Azure subscriptions at the root scope

Authentication Method - Microsoft Authenticator - Show geographic location in push and passwordless notifications.
Authentication Method - Microsoft Authenticator - Included users/groups to show geographic location in push and passwordless notifications.
Default Authorization Settings - Enabled Self service password reset for administrators.
Default Authorization Settings - Guest invite restrictions.
Default Authorization Settings - Sign-up for email based subscription.
Default Authorization Settings - User can join the tenant by email validation.
Default Authorization Settings - Guest user access.
Default Authorization Settings - User consent policy assigned for applications.
Default Authorization Settings - Allow user consent on risk-based apps.
Default Authorization Settings - Default User Role Permissions - Allowed to create Apps.

Authentication Method - SMS - Use for sign-in.

Default Settings - Consent Policy Settings - Group owner consent for apps accessing data.
Default Settings - Consent Policy Settings - Block user consent for risky apps.
Default Settings - Consent Policy Settings - Users can request admin consent to apps they are unable to consent to.
Consent Framework - Admin Consent Request - Policy to enable or disable admin consent request feature.
Consent Framework - Admin Consent Request - Reviewers will receive email notifications for requests.
Consent Framework - Admin Consent Request - Reviewers will receive email notifications when admin consent requests are about to expire.
Consent Framework - Admin Consent Request - Consent request duration (days).
Default Settings - Password Rule Settings - Password Protection - Mode.
Default Settings - Password Rule Settings - Password Protection - Enable password protection on Windows Server Active Directory.
Default Settings - Password Rule Settings - Enforce custom list.
Default Settings - Password Rule Settings - Smart Lockout - Lockout duration in seconds.
Default Settings - Password Rule Settings - Smart Lockout - Lockout threshold.
Default Settings - Classification and M365 Groups - M365 groups - Allow Guests to become Group Owner.
Default Settings - Classification and M365 Groups - M365 groups - Allow Guests to have access to groups content.
At least one Conditional Access policy is configured with device compliance.
App management restrictions on applications and service principals is configured and enabled.
At least one Conditional Access policy is configured with All Apps.
At least one Conditional Access policy is configured with All Apps and All Users.
All Conditional Access policies are configured to exclude at least one emergency/break glass account or group.

At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to enforce sign-in frequency for non-corporate devices.
At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to enable application enforced restrictions.
All Conditional Access policies are configured to exclude directory synchronization accounts or do not scope them.
Security Defaults are enabled.
All users utilizing a P1 license should be licensed.
All users utilizing a P2 license should be licensed.
Entra Recommendation - Protect your tenant with Insider Risk condition in Conditional Access policy
Entra Recommendation - Do not allow users to grant consent to unreliable applications
Entra Recommendation - Enable policy to block legacy authentication
Entra Recommendation - Require multifactor authentication for administrative roles

Restrict non-admin users from creating security groups.

Conditional access policies should not use the deprecated Approved Client App grant.
Soft- and hard-matching of synchronized objects should be blocked.
Mailboxes should not send outbound mails using the .onmicrosoft.com domain.
Third Party Entra Apps should only have explicitly assigned users instead of All Users.
MOERA SHOULD NOT be used for sent mail.
App registrations with privileged API permissions should not have owners
App registrations with highly privileged directory roles should not have owners
Privileged API permissions on service principals should not remain unused
Credentials, tokens, or cookies from highly privileged users should not be exposed on vulnerable endpoints
Hybrid users should not be assigned Entra ID role assignments
Ensure Delicensing Resiliency is enabled
Seamless Single SignOn should be disabled for all domains in EntraID Connect servers.
Pending approvals for Critical Asset Management should not be present
Devices should not share both critical and non-critical user credentials.
Devices should not be publicly exposed with remotely exploitable, highly likely to be exploited, high or critical severity CVE's.
Devices with critical credentials should be protected by TPM.
Devices with critical credentials should be protected by Credential Guard.
Global administrator role should not be added as local administrator on the device during Microsoft Entra join
Registering user should not be added as local administrator on the device during Microsoft Entra join
Intune APNS certificate should be valid for more than 30 days
Apple Automated Device Enrollment Tokens should be valid for more than 30 days
Apple Volume Purchase Program Tokens should be valid for more than 30 days
Android Enterprise Account Connection should be healthy
Intune Multi Admin approval should be configured
Certificate Connectors should be healthy and running supported versions
Mobile Threat Defense Connectors should be healthy
Windows Diagnostic Data Processing should be enabled
Intune Audit Logs should be retained
Default Branding Profile should be customized
Windows Feature Update Policy Settings should not reference end of support builds
Intune RBAC groups should be protected by Restricted Management Administrative Units or Role Assignable groups
MDM Authority should be set to Microsoft Intune
Catalog resources must have valid roles (no stale app roles or deleted SPNs)
Access packages and catalogs should not reference deleted groups
Access packages should not have inactive or orphaned assignment policies
Access package approval workflows must have valid approvers
No catalog should contain resources without any associated access packages
High privileged user should be linked to an identity
Privileged user accounts should not remain enabled when the linked primary account is disabled
AI agents should not be shared with broad access control policies
AI agents should require user authentication
AI agents should not have risky HTTP configurations
AI agents should not send email with AI-controlled inputs
Published AI agents should not be dormant
AI agents should avoid using author (maker) authentication for tools
AI agents should not have hard-coded credentials in topics
AI agents should not use MCP server tools without review
AI agents with generative orchestration should have custom instructions
AI agents should not have orphaned ownership
Ensure BitLocker full disk encryption is configured via Intune
Do not sync krbtgt_AzureAD to Entra ID

Behavior Monitoring should be enabled
Cloud Protection should be enabled
Email Scanning should be enabled
Script Scanning should be enabled
Real-time Monitoring should be enabled

Scanning Network Files should be enabled
CPU Load Factor should be optimized (20-30%)
Scan should be scheduled
Quick Scan Time configuration is not required
Signatures should be checked before scan
Cloud Block Level should be High or higher
Cloud Extended Timeout should be 30-50 seconds
Signature Update Interval should be 1-4 hours
PUA Protection should be enabled
Network Protection should be enabled
Local Admin Merge should be disabled
Real-Time Scan Direction should cover both directions
Cleaned Malware should be retained for at least 30 days
Catch-up Full Scan should be disabled
Catch-up Quick Scan should be disabled
